TextENGNew: The classroom felt quiet since morning.
Whether it was actually a quiet day, or whether it just felt that way because Mion was absent -- I couldn't tell.
There was no doubt that Mion had a strong presence... At any rate, she was still out.
Apparently, her cold just wouldn't go away.
When Mion first started missing school, we'd suspended club activities.
changeReason: Old says "usually suspended any time Mion is out" (general rule); new says "when Mion first started missing, we'd suspended" (specific event).

TextENGNew: But as her absence dragged on, we gradually started up again, without anyone explicitly suggesting it.
changeReason: New adds "without anyone explicitly suggesting it" -- spontaneity detail old omitted.

TextENGNew: When it came down to it, everyone loved our club activities.
And we all wanted Mion back so we could make a ruckus together again... as soon as possible.
Without Mion around, I should've been able to win easily.
But lately, I'd been on a losing streak.
I couldn't get Mion out of my head. I just couldn't concentrate.
And that wasn't limited to club activities.
My entire daily life had been like this recently.
Today, I'd managed to come in dead last once again.
changeReason: Old says "particularly low point"; new says "dead last once again" -- different characterization.

TextENGNew: With my consecutive last-place finishes, for better or worse, the penalty game ideas had dried up.
changeReason: Old says "stories left me too exhausted for a penalty"; new says "penalty game ideas had dried up" -- completely different meaning.

TextENGNew: Today's penalty was deferred as homework for next time.
So that was how I managed to escape a penalty today.
Once again, it was just Rena and me walking home.
Even with Mion gone, the higurashi cicadas kept crying just the same.
No matter who disappears...
These cicadas would keep crying as though nothing had changed from yesterday.
Right...?
Isn't that how it works...?
``What's wrong, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS17/02/110200096. You've got that faraway look in your eyes.``
``Huh? What?``
``Rena's talking to you, you know?@k@vS17/02/110200098. Are you thinking about something? ...Something?``
``No, not really...``
``Are you worried about the next penalty game? ...Are you?``
``Y-yeah, something like that...``
Rena was chattering away more than usual today.
But I couldn't quite keep up with the conversation... Maybe she was picking up on something strange about me.
changeReason: Old says Rena "wasn't acting like herself"; new says narrator couldn't keep up -- different subject of oddness.

TextENGNew: Or maybe there just wasn't enough room in my head to process Rena's small talk.
``...Come to think of it, you've been losing a lot in club activities lately, Keiichi-kun.``
``Haha. Yeah, I just can't seem to get in the groove...``
``... Then, how about... pickles?``
``Huh? What did you say?``
``I said, maybe I should bring you some pickled vegetables to perk you up.@k@vS17/02/110200103. You said they were delicious before. ...Before.``
``Oh, yeah... thanks.``
Mion. No, the story I'd heard from Mion had been occupying my thoughts ever since.
``Keiichi-kun? ...Hey, Keiichi-kun.``
Rena was saying something to me again.
I should probably start paying attention before I hurt her feelings.
But concentrating on command isn't as easy as it sounds.
``By the way.@k@vS17/02/110200107. --Have you heard anything about Tomitake-san?``
Tomitake...
Tomitake-san...
...Tomitake-san!!
``...Why do you ask?``
``Someone came to Rena's house.@k@vS17/02/110200109. A police officer.``
Something about Tomitake-san...
changeReason: Old says "Tomitake-san's story" (known); new says "Something about Tomitake-san" (unknown topic) -- different knowledge state.

TextENGNew: The police went to Rena's...
changeReason: Old says "Why would the police..."; new says "The police went to Rena's..." -- different incomplete thoughts.

TextENGNew: ...What did they ask her?
changeReason: Old asks "Be asking Rena about that?"; new asks "What did they ask her?" -- why vs what.

TextENGNew: ``Rena, what kind of things did they--``
The moment I started to ask, my voice was drowned out by a car horn.
A black car had pulled up right beside Rena and me.
The imposing vehicle radiated an unsettling aura, and alarm bells started going off in my head.
A middle-aged man stepped out of the car.
``Aah, excuse me, excuse me.``
``Y-yes?``
``You're Keiichi Maebara-san... correct?``
``Th-that's right.``
Who was this old guy...?
I'd never seen him before in my life.@k And yet, he already knew my name.
On top of that, there was something decidedly unusual about his aura.
He had the build of a slightly pudgy middle-aged man, but he clearly wasn't all fat.
He gave the impression that when push came to shove, those muscles could spring into action.
His build brought to mind a seasoned judo practitioner... or something like that.
changeReason: Old says "elderly judo master"; new says "seasoned judo practitioner." Japanese 壮年 means prime of life, not elderly.

TextENGNew: His voice was soft, but there was no carelessness or leniency in his gaze.
He was the type whose profession was hard to guess. Or rather, I had a feeling there was a specific line of work that produced a lot of men like him...
``I'd like to have a word with you... if you wouldn't mind?``
Being asked to talk out of the blue like this... what was the best way to respond...?
Just then, I felt a tug on my sleeve. It was Rena.
``We're going shopping together today~ hau~``
The remark was so sudden that for a second I couldn't figure out what Rena was getting at.
But her whisper told me everything I needed to know.
``...That's him.``
When Rena said 'that's him,' there was no doubt who she meant.
The detective who had visited Rena.@k ...And she was trying to get me away from this man.
Should I play along with Rena and refuse the man's request...?
``Nahahaha.@k@vS17/11/110700005. Well now, it seems I'm quite the nuisance.``
``N-no... not at all, ahahaha...``
``Oh no, my apologies.@k@vS17/11/110700007. I'll try to keep this as brief as possible, all right?@k@vS17/11/110700008. ...Nfufufufu!``
``S-sure...@k@vS17/01/110100217. But, what is this about?``
``My name is Ooishi, from the Okinomiya police station.``
``...I see. Ooishi-san.``
``By any chance, has Rena Ryuugu-san here told you anything?``
``...N-nothing in particular.@k@vS17/01/110100220. Right, Rena?``
``...I haven't told him anything.@k@vS17/02/110200113. I was told not to discuss it with anyone.``

``Nfufufufu.@k@vS17/11/110700012. That's admirable of you.@k@vS17/11/110700013. Very helpful to us.@k@vS17/11/110700014. ...So, I was hoping Maebara-san here could answer some of the same questions.@k@vS17/11/110700015. Would that be all right?``
``...Does it have to be right now?``
``We need to go shopping, right? Right?``
Rena continued putting up resistance for me.
But this detective named Ooishi, while soft-spoken, clearly wasn't going to take no for an answer.
changeReason: Old says Ooishi was "reluctant to give a yes or no answer"; new says he "wasn't going to take no for an answer" -- opposite meanings.

TextENGNew: ``Nfufufufu.@k@vS17/11/110700017. Ryuugu-san, I understand how you feel, but could I borrow Maebara-san just for today?@k@vS17/11/110700018. How about it, Maebara-san?@k@vS17/11/110700019. I'd appreciate your cooperation.``
I concluded there was no avoiding this man, so I gave Rena a subtle look.
Rena looked slightly uneasy, but she gave me a small nod in return.
``All right, I'll talk with you.@k@vS17/01/110100223. Sorry, Rena.@k@vS17/01/110100224. Go on home without me.``
``...Okay, I understand.@k@vS17/02/110200116. See you tomorrow. ...Okay?``
``Much appreciated.@k@vS17/11/110700021. Sorry to intrude, but this is part of the job.@k@vS17/11/110700022. Nfufufufu.``
After seeing Rena off, I climbed into Ooishi-san's car.
The inside of the car was pleasantly cool. Actually, it was borderline cold.
Having air conditioning meant this had to be a high-end car.@k At least, my dad's car didn't have it.
``Let me know if it gets too cold, all right?@k@vS17/11/110700024. I tend to blast the A/C.``
Everything was proceeding entirely at Ooishi-san's pace.
I felt a prick of irritation, but at this point, there seemed to be nothing I could do about it.
``Sorry about springing this on you.@k@vS17/11/110700026. Nahahaha.``
I got the eerie sense that this detective could read everything going on inside my head.
At this point, I had absolutely no idea what to make of him.
But one thing I vaguely sensed: this was not someone I wanted as an enemy.
With practiced ease, Ooishi-san pulled a notebook from his breast pocket, flipped through the pages, and plucked out a photograph tucked inside.
The photo showed... what looked like an ID photo of some kind. A man's expressionless face stared back at me.
My body gave a start.
Because I recognized that face.
``If you know anything about this man, please tell me.``
``According to Rena Ryuugu-san, you're already acquainted with this man, aren't you, Maebara-san?``
``...Uh... that's... Tomitake-san......``
Why would a detective be coming to me about Tomitake-san...?
What was his objective...?
``Incidentally, this man has been missing since the night of the festival.``
``Could you tell me about the last time you saw him?``
``The last time I saw him was on the night of the festival...``
``And you haven't seen him since?``
Ooishi-san jotted something in his notebook as he listened. What was he writing? Investigation notes, probably.
``So... Maebara-san.@k@vS17/11/110700034. Can you think of any reason why this man might have disappeared?``

``Did anyone hold a grudge against him? Was he mixed up in some kind of relationship trouble...?@k@vS17/11/110700036. Even the smallest detail is fine.@k@vS17/11/110700037. Please, tell me anything you know about this man.``
``I've also heard he had a rather clingy personality, and that women found him quite off-putting...@k@vS17/11/110700039. Maybe he was doing something stalker-like and earned someone's grudge?``
``Tomitake-san isn't like that at all!``
The way Ooishi-san spoke about a dead man was downright cruel.
His baseless accusations had gone too far. It felt like desecration of the dead, and I was filled with righteous indignation.
But I immediately regretted getting so heated over it, and added more calmly,

``No... I'm sorry.@k@vS17/01/110100235. I got carried away...@k@vS17/01/110100236. But really, Tomitake-san was well-liked by everyone.@k@vS17/01/110100237. I can't think of any reason someone would want to kill--``
Right then, Ooishi-san's eyebrow twitched.
That was the moment I realized my slip-up.
``...Oh my~?@k@vS17/11/110700041. And how do you know he was killed?``
By the time I thought 'oh no,' it was already too late.
``I said he 'disappeared.'@k@vS17/11/110700043. But just now, you distinctly said he was 'killed.'``
Oh no! ...My regret must have shown on my face too. The corner of Ooishi-san's mouth twisted into a grin.
From my momentary reaction, Ooishi-san seemed to have reached a certain conviction.
``It's true, Tomitake-san is dead.@k@vS17/11/110700045. However, this case is supposed to be confidential -- known only to the police.@k@vS17/11/110700046. So, who told you about it?``
I was at a loss for words.
I absolutely couldn't say I'd heard it from Mion.
Given that Mion had told me the police already suspected her, dropping her name here would make her situation critical.
As I sat in silence, Ooishi-san pressed on, not letting up.
``Are you familiar with... Mion Sonozaki-san?``
Hearing Mion's name so suddenly, I felt the blood drain from my face.
``W-well, she's my classmate, so...``
I kept my answer vague. I just wanted to get through this as quickly as possible... I was desperate to get away.
``Nahahaha.@k@vS17/11/110700049. That's not quite what I meant.``
I'd tried to answer as calmly as possible to hide my agitation, but Ooishi-san kept fixed on me with those sharp eyes that seemed to see right through my heart.
Even while letting out hearty laughter, his eyes weren't smiling at all.
``Th-then what do you mean...?``
``Let's call it her 'true face.'@k@vS17/11/110700051. What I mean is, do you know what kind of person Mion Sonozaki truly is...?``
When I stepped out of Ooishi-san's car, the temperature difference barely registered. I just stood there in a daze.
The Mion that Ooishi-san described was a Mion I didn't know.
His story began during the dam opposition movement.
Apparently, during those protests, Mion had been thrown into a holding cell multiple times.
She'd been at the forefront of the village-wide uproar against the dam.
And according to Ooishi-san, Mion had been the de facto leader of the resistance.
She'd directed the opposition activities, commanding subordinates from the Sonozaki family's... underworld connections -- yakuza or some kind of organized crime outfit.
Even more astonishing was the claim that she was now the Sonozaki family's effective leader.
What the Sonozaki family represented in this area...
Having heard that much... I was shaking.
Mion was the de facto leader of the Sonozaki family...
Mion was... the ruler of this village...
And that same Mion had told me about the dangers surrounding the Sonozaki family...
The very person who stood at the top of that family...
Why...?
...What was the point of that conversation at the library...?
Could it have been... a warning directed at me...?
By personally revealing the Sonozaki family's dark side...
Was she saying that if I talked too much, even I could be eliminated... so keep my mouth shut -- is that it?
Mion Sonozaki...
I'd thought of her as a reliable older friend with natural leadership -- just a good companion.
But in reality...
At that moment, a violent chill shot down my spine.
I couldn't tell if it was from the car's air conditioning, or from something else entirely.
For now, I should just go home...
I wanted nothing more than to curl up in my bed.
I walked home as fast as I could.
I'd sort through everything once I was inside.@k First, I just wanted to feel some vague sense of 'safety.'
When the gate of my house came into view, I finally felt a wave of relief wash over me.
And just as I was about to reach for the gate... a voice came from behind me.
``Keiichi-kun.``
``Waah!``
The person who'd called out to me was, of all people, Rena.
``R-Rena!@k@vS17/01/110100243. What are you doing in front of my house...?!``
Rena didn't answer my question. She just kept staring at me in silence.
``...You sure took your time.``
When I'd gotten into Ooishi-san's car, Rena was supposed to have headed home.
Had she not gone home at all? Had she been waiting in front of my house this entire time?
``That's right.@k@vS17/02/110200120. I've been waiting right here for you, Keiichi-kun, this whole time.``
It had been quite a while since I'd gotten into Ooishi-san's car...
This was strange...
This felt less like 'waiting' and more like an 'ambush'...
And for such a long time, too...
Wasn't Rena's behavior a little... abnormal...?
``I-is that right?@k@vS17/01/110100245. Sorry about that.@k@vS17/01/110100246. ...That detective just wouldn't let me leave...``
``Is that so?@k@vS17/02/110200122. How awful.``
Rena's expression seemed to grow harder by the second.
I couldn't tell if her displeasure was directed at Ooishi-san, or at me.
``...A-anyway... did you need something?@k@vS17/01/110100248. You've been waiting here a while, haven't you...?``
``I was worried about you, Keiichi-kun... so I waited.``
Rena's tone was too hard, too cold... and too forceful. It made me recoil.
...Had I said something to upset her?
Was I overthinking it? ...Rena had seemed displeased when we'd parted earlier. Was this just a continuation of that?
Something about Rena felt unusually on edge.
...It might be best not to provoke her right now.
As I thought that and lowered my eyes, something occurred to me.
Come to think of it... what had Rena been worried about, exactly...?
Ooishi-san was a detective.@k He'd never pose any threat to my safety. ...So what about me had Rena been worried about?
...If she was worried about me being with a detective... then it wasn't my 'safety' she was concerned about.
What on earth was Rena so worried about that she'd stake out my house like this...?
``Th-thanks.@k@vS17/01/110100250. But you really don't need to worry about me...``
``What did he ask you?``
Rena's sharp gaze locked directly onto mine.
Her tone had a forcefulness that said: you're not getting away.
...Was Rena trying to find out what I'd told the detective?
Why...?
It hit me.
The only thing I knew that the police couldn't find out about was... the Sonozaki family...!
No way... It couldn't be...
Thoughts I didn't want to think kept spinning inside my head.
Why would Rena need to be concerned about the Sonozaki family...?!
...The answer was obvious. It meant Rena was connected to Mion.
If Mion had ordered Rena to keep tabs on me... everything fell into place.
A creeping sensation crawled up my spine.
I hadn't said anything. Nothing that would be inconvenient for the Sonozaki family -- nothing at all...!
I didn't know anything dangerous to begin with. That's right -- I hadn't heard anything from Mion, and I didn't know anything.
So I'd be fine... Calm down, Keiichi Maebara!
I took a small, deep breath and answered Rena in the lightest tone I could manage.
``...Oh, it was about the same stuff you told me about.``
``About Tomitake-san...?``
``Yeah.@k@vS17/01/110100253. Nothing else in particular...``
``Really?@k@vS17/02/110200127. Your face looks pale, Keiichi-kun... Are you okay?``
Rena sharpened her gaze even further, peering into the depths of my eyes.
...Rena was suspicious of me.
But I really didn't know anything. ...There was no reason to be rattled.
I'm fine. Stay calm.
``D-does it?@k@vS17/01/110100255. I'm fine, really.@k@vS17/01/110100256. He honestly didn't ask about anything else.``
``...Not even about Mii-chan?``
Mion...!
Why?! Why was Mion's name coming out of Rena's mouth? Why bring up Mion here, now, of all times...?!
So Rena really was connected to Mion...
That conviction did nothing except ratchet my tension higher.
...But it was fine. Even if Rena and Mion were working together, I truly hadn't said anything to the detective...
``Mion?@k@vS17/01/110100258. No, he didn't really ask about her.``
``Oh?@k@vS17/02/110200130. ...Is that really true?@k@vS17/02/110200131. ...Because when he talked to me, he asked about Mii-chan.``
With those words, Rena fixed me with a skeptical, searching stare.
Her eyes seemed to be sizing me up... For a split second, I wavered over whether I should tell the truth.
...No. Changing my story now would only make things worse. I had no choice but to play dumb until the end.
``Maybe it's because you two are close? Girl talk, you know?``
``You think so?@k@vS17/02/110200133. You really think so?@k@vS17/02/110200134. I wonder about that~.@k@vS17/02/110200135. But of course, Rena didn't tell him anything.``
``Y-yeah, of course...``
I had no idea what she meant by 'of course.' But all I could do was give harmless, noncommittal answers and try to get through this.
``...Besides, I'm no traitor.``
``No one's ever thought of you as a traitor...``
``...Right.@k@vS17/02/110200138. ...Besides, making Mii-chan angry would be scary.@k@vS17/02/110200139. ...Ahahahahahahahaha.``
There wasn't a shred of amusement in Rena's laughter.
If anything, it sounded mocking. Like she was sneering at a coward.
...Was this a threat?
Was Rena trying to force me into silence?
I didn't talk! I haven't said a word, and I don't even know anything worth saying! I'm not involved!
...I was desperate to plead my case to Rena.
...But blurting that out would only make me look more suspicious.
First Mion, and now Rena too -- they were both trying to intimidate me...?
They were watching me to make sure I didn't do anything rash...? Were they planning to keep watching me... from now on?
I didn't want to think about what came next.
As long as every day was fun, that was enough for me. I didn't want to think about anything else. ...I just wanted no part of any of this insanity.
I need to get inside, now!
I need to send Rena home, now!
``Well, it's getting late... we should probably head home.``
``...Are you trying to run away?``
Without a beat, Rena's cold voice pierced into me.
``Wh-what makes you say that...?``
``Fine.@k@vS17/02/110200142. ...Well then, goodbye.``
She said it with startling curtness, then spun on her heel and strode away.
It felt like I'd been cast aside. I stood there, dumbfounded.
I wanted to call out to Rena, but I didn't know what to say.
All I could do was numbly watch her retreating figure.
Rena's silhouette grew smaller in the distance... but after just a few steps, she suddenly stopped... and turned back to face me.
With eyes full of suspicion, chilling to the bone...
``--Keiichi-kun.@k@vS17/02/110200144. Everything's all right... isn't it?``
The force of her words left me speechless.
Rena's eyes were the kind that could see through my heart and bind it in place.
...If you betray us, you have no idea what will happen, do you?
That's what Rena's eyes were saying.
Rena turned her back once more and began walking home.
Once her figure had completely vanished from sight, I bolted into the house as if fleeing for my life.
